name: AWS WAF Managed Rule Extension Pack
description: Custom rule group designed to complement AWS Managed Rules with detections for advanced SQL injection, obfuscated XSS payloads, and suspicious query string behaviors.
risk_mitigated: Query string abuse and anomaly patterns
deployment_notes: Deploy in COUNT mode first to observe potential false positives.
Review logs in AWS WAF logging (CloudWatch or Kinesis) before switching to BLOCK.
Tune regex patterns if your application legitimately uses encoded script fragments or complex query strings.
tuning_notes: Whitelist known internal scanners or monitoring tools that may trigger encoded payload detections.
Adjust query string length thresholds for APIs with large query parameters.
Combine with AWS Managed Rule Groups such as AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet and AWSManagedRulesKnownBadInputsRuleSet.

rules.name: advanced-sqli-patterns
rules.description: Detects SQL injection attempts using comment obfuscation, concatenation tricks, and union-based enumeration.

rules.name: obfuscated-xss-payloads
rules.description: Detects encoded or obfuscated cross-site scripting payloads in query parameters.

rules.name: suspicious-query-string-anomaly
rules.description: Blocks abnormal query strings that indicate fuzzing, payload spraying, or exploitation attempts.

monitoring_guidance: Monitor AWS WAF logs for rule names advanced-sqli-patterns and obfuscated-xss-payloads.
Investigate spikes in suspicious-query-string-anomaly as they may indicate automated scanning.
Correlate with ALB or CloudFront access logs for full request context.
